topic.what: Comprehensive review of gastric mucosal defense mechanisms — the mucus-bicarbonate barrier, prostaglandin-mediated cytoprotection, mucosal blood flow regulation, epithelial restitution, and their disruption by NSAIDs
topic.how: Integrative physiological review synthesizing decades of research on the multi-layered gastric defense system, from pre-epithelial (mucus-bicarbonate-phospholipid layer) through epithelial (tight junctions, restitution, proliferation) to sub-epithelial (mucosal blood flow, sensory nerves) defenses
topic.why_matters: Understanding the layered architecture of gastric mucosal defense is essential for explaining why NSAIDs cause gastropathy (prostaglandin depletion compromises multiple defense layers simultaneously) and for designing targeted gastroprotective strategies

characteristics.what_it_measures: Multi-layered conceptual framework of gastric mucosal protection
characteristics.value: Three-tier defense: pre-epithelial (mucus-bicarbonate-phospholipid), epithelial (tight junctions, restitution, bicarbonate secretion), sub-epithelial (mucosal blood flow, sensory nerves, immune cells)
characteristics.vs_baseline: Integrates previously fragmented understanding of individual defense mechanisms into a unified model
characteristics.context: Physiological Reviews article providing definitive synthesis of gastric mucosal defense physiology

good_for: Understanding the physiological basis of gastric mucosal integrity
Explaining the mechanism of NSAID-induced gastropathy at each defense layer
Rational design of mucosal protective agents (prostaglandin analogs, mucosal protectants)
Foundation for understanding how H. pylori disrupts mucosal defense
not_for: Clinical management protocols for peptic ulcer disease
Gastric neoplasia pathogenesis beyond mucosal injury
requires: Background in gastrointestinal physiology and prostaglandin biochemistry
Understanding of arachidonic acid metabolism and COX isoenzymes

novelty: Definitive synthesis of the multi-layered gastric mucosal defense system with emphasis on prostaglandin-dependent and prostaglandin-independent mechanisms
Detailed mechanistic explanation of how NSAID-induced prostaglandin depletion simultaneously compromises multiple defense layers
Integrates sensory nerve (CGRP, substance P) and nitric oxide contributions to mucosal defense, expanding beyond the classic mucus-bicarbonate model
open_problems: Relative contribution of each defense layer to overall mucosal integrity is difficult to quantify in vivo
Mechanisms of epithelial restitution (rapid migration) vs proliferation (slower renewal) are incompletely understood
How to restore mucosal defense without eliminating the therapeutic anti-inflammatory effect of NSAIDs
Role of the gastric microbiome (beyond H. pylori) in mucosal defense is unexplored
